title: ［突發］向西方國家(包括：英國、美國、歐盟成員國)發公開信呼籲禁止向香港警察提供防暴裝備

replies.msg:
我哋今次行動已經計劃甚久，早喺https://lihkg.com/thread/1221312/ 已經討論我哋行動。宜家另開新po希望各位可以助我哋一臂之力，幫手向各國發公開信。

我哋今次發起呢個活動係因為眼見我哋義民喺六月一連串反送中活動中警方一連串行為，包括：用過度武力鎮壓、濫用警權，胡亂進行截查等行為，以及已逝去義士為喚醒香港市民而不惜自我犠牲，感到心心不忿，所以發起公開信活動向西方國家(包括：英國、美國、歐盟成員國等)呼籲禁止向香港警察提供防暴裝備。稍後我哋都會向加拿大發起公開信活動呼籲加拿大禁止向香港警察提供防暴裝備。早前，巴打Fuck_Labour已經向英國MP陳情，並獲回函。(詳情請見：https://lih.kg/1255890)可見公開信活動可喚起英國政界關注。

為此，我地希望連登各位手足可以抽小小時間，以電郵方式向各國呼籲停止向香港警察提供武器（詳情可見二、三、四樓，入面會有公開信同收件人電郵地址。我哋亦會因應情況向連仔請求解鎖。

最後，要感謝J.Spearing發起今次活動、Fuck_Labour幫我同J.Spearing向相熟MP陳情。此外，亦多謝攪炒巴提供發起公開信嘅靈感同埋整理警方罪證。

當大家因爲連日嘅事而氣餒時，唔好唔記得，世界只有我哋係香港人。共勉之。

美國國會及行政當局中國委員會及美中經濟與安全審查委員會委員公開信內容，請大家send 去以下電郵地址(下面有條onedrive link，最右手有個掣，click入去就可以選擇”save as PDF”，然後attach 落去封email度)：



To: infocecc@mail.house.gov, contact@uscc.gov
cc: UNODA-web@un.org, hrwpress@hrw.org, report@aiusa.org

Dear Commissioners of the Congressional-Executive Commission on China and the U.S.-China Economic and Security Review Commission,

AN OPEN LETTER TO THE US CONGRESS FROM CONCERNED HONG KONG CITIZENS REQUESTING A SUSPENSION OF ISSUANCE OF ARMS EXPORT LICENSE TO HKPF WITH AN IMMEDIATE EFFECT

1. We are a group of ordinary Hong Kong citizens who attended a series of mass demonstrations that took place between 9 June and 12 June 2019 against the Hong Kong SAR Government’s proposal to pass the Fugitive Offenders and Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters Legislation (Amendment) Bill 2019 (commonly known as “the Extradition Bill”). The demonstrations were conducted mostly in a peaceful manner in which only a small number of confrontations occurred. However, as attached in Appendix I, the Hong Kong Police Force (HKPF) has used unreasonably excessive force against protesters and the press, which is in violation of including, but not limited to Article 7, 17, 19(2), and 21 of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights.

2. We understand that despite the handover of Hong Kong’s sovereignty to the People’s Republic China, a communist regime, the West had still been continuously supplying weapons to HKPF under the reason that HKPF could use the Western weapons as a means to protect Hong Kong residents. However, such considerations have been proved wrong as HKPF was using them to oppress Hong Kong residents, not to protect. In fact, Heckler & Koch, a Germany company, refused to supply MP5 over the concerns that the weapons would fall into the hands of autocratic regimes, of which the Hong Kong Government is becoming.

3. Therefore, we sincerely urge the US Congress to take practical action convincing the US Government to immediately suspend the issuance of arms export license to HKPF and ensure all companies in the US including, but not limited to those listed on Appendix II will not export weapons to HKPF.

Yours truly,
A group of Hong Kong citizens


https://1drv.ms/w/s!AuXDHQ_cRAr3gmIaeo5PQdw3Oulu?e=rQlBww

英國下議院 Committees on Arms Export Controls Chair公開信內容，請大家send 去以下電郵地址(下面有條onedrive link，最右手有個掣，click入去就可以選擇”save as PDF”，然後attach 落去封email度)：



To: caeccom@parliament.uk, graham.jones.mp@parliament.uk

cc: UNODA-web@un.org, tilianm@hrw.org, media@caat.org.uk, sct@amnesty.org.uk, info@omegaresearchfoundation.org

30 June 2019

Graham P Jones MP
Chair, Committees on Arm Export Controls,
House of Commons,
London, SW1A 0AA

Dear Mr. Graham P Jones MP ,

AN OPEN LETTER TO HER MAJESTY’S GOVERNMENT FROM CONCERNED HONG KONG CITIZENS REQUESTING A SUSPENSION OF ISSUANCE OF ARMS EXPORT LICENSE TO HKPF WITH AN IMMEDIATE EFFECT

1. We are a group of ordinary Hong Kong citizens who attended a series of mass demonstrations that took place between 9 June and 12 June 2019 against the Hong Kong SAR Government’s proposal to pass the Fugitive Offenders and Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters Legislation (Amendment) Bill 2019 (commonly known as “the Extradition Bill”). The demonstrations were conducted mostly in a peaceful manner in which only a small number of confrontations occurred. However, as attached in Appendix I, the Hong Kong Police Force (HKPF) has used unreasonably excessive force against protesters and the press, which is in violation of including, but not limited to Article 7, 17, 19(2), and 21 of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights.

2. We understand that despite the handover of Hong Kong’s sovereignty to the People’s Republic China, a communist regime, the West had still been continuously supplying weapons to HKPF under the reason that HKPF could use the Western weapons as a means to protect Hong Kong residents. However, such considerations have been proved wrong as HKPF was using them to oppress Hong Kong residents, not to protect. In fact, Heckler & Koch, a Germany company, refused to supply MP5 over the concerns that the weapons would fall into the hands of autocratic regimes, of which the Hong Kong Government is becoming.

3. Therefore, we sincerely urge Her Majesty’s government to immediately suspend the issuance of arms export license to HKPF and ensure all companies in the UK including, but not limited to those listed on Appendix II will not export weapons to HKPF.

Yours sincerely,
A group of Hong Kong citizens

To: afet-secretariat@ep.europa.edu, droi-secretariat@ep.europa.eu

30 June 2019

Dear Mr. Pier Antonio Panzeri and Mr. David McAllister,


AN OPEN LETTER TO THE EUROPEAN UNION FROM CONCERNED HONG KONG CITIZENS DEMANDING A WEAPON EMBARGO AGAINST HONG KONG GOVERNMENT WITH AN IMMEDIATE EFFECT

1. We are a group of ordinary Hong Kong citizens who attended a series of mass demonstrations that took place between 9 June and 12 June 2019 against the Hong Kong SAR Government’s proposal to pass the Fugitive Offenders and Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters Legislation (Amendment) Bill 2019 (commonly known as “the Extradition Bill”). The demonstrations were conducted mostly in a peaceful manner in which only a small number of confrontations occurred. However, as attached in Appendix I, the Hong Kong Police Force (HKPF) has used unreasonably excessive force against protesters and the press, which is in violation of including, but not limited to Article 7, 17, 19(2), and 21 of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights.

2. We understand that despite the handover of Hong Kong’s sovereignty to the People’s Republic China, a communist regime, the West had still been continuously supplying weapons to HKPF under the jurisdiction that HKPF could use the Western weapons as a means to protect Hong Kong residents. However, such considerations have been proved wrong as HKPF was using them to oppress Hong Kong residents, not to protect. In fact, Heckler & Koch, a Germany company, refused to supply MP5 over the concerns that the weapons would fall into the hands of autocratic regimes, of which the Hong Kong Government is becoming.

3. Therefore, we sincerely urge the European Union to enforce a weapon embargo against Hong Kong Government and ensure all companies in all EU countries will not export weapons to Hong Kong.

Yours sincerely,
A group of Hong Kong citizens
